name: IBS
relevance_signal: Recurrent abdominal pain, bloating, cramping, diarrhoea, constipation, or alternating bowel habits — often diagnosed after other causes have been ruled out

name: Lactase deficiency
relevance_signal: **Bloating, gas, loose stool** within 1–2 hours of drinking milk, eating yoghurt or soft cheese — but you tolerate hard aged cheeses fine.

name: Sucrase-isomaltase deficiency
relevance_signal: Symptoms after **sweet foods, bread, pasta** even on a low-FODMAP diet. Bloating and diarrhoea from table sugar, fruit juice, or starches that most people tolerate.

name: Fructose malabsorption
relevance_signal: **Bloating and loose stool** after fruit, honey, or fruit juice — especially apples, mangoes, and anything with high-fructose corn syrup.

name: Sorbitol / polyol intolerance
relevance_signal: **Gas, bloating, urgency** after stone fruits (peaches, plums, cherries), sugar-free gum, or diet products containing xylitol or sorbitol.

name: Bile acid malabsorption
relevance_signal: **Watery, urgent diarrhoea** — especially in the morning. Symptoms began or worsened after gallbladder removal. Responds partially to a low-fat diet.

name: Exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
relevance_signal: **Fatty, pale, foul-smelling stools** that float. Unintentional weight loss. Bloating and discomfort after fatty meals. Worse with a history of pancreatitis or heavy alcohol use.

name: SIBO
relevance_signal: **Bloating that worsens through the day**, peaks after carbs, and improves on antibiotics or a low-FODMAP diet.

name: Hydrogen SIBO
relevance_signal: **Bloating that starts within 1–2 hours of eating**, especially carbohydrates. Symptoms even after clean low-FODMAP meals. Symptom relief with antibiotics in the past.

name: Methane SIBO
relevance_signal: **Constipation dominant** with bloating. Slow transit. Symptoms worse with fibre. Hard, infrequent stools despite adequate hydration.

name: Hydrogen sulphide SIBO
relevance_signal: **Rotten egg-smelling gas**. Diarrhoea dominant. Symptoms worse after sulphur-rich foods — eggs, meat, cruciferous vegetables.

name: Gut dysbiosis
relevance_signal: **Chronic, low-grade gut symptoms** without a clear trigger. Worsened by antibiotics. Improved somewhat by probiotics. No single food trigger but general instability.

name: Parasitic infection
relevance_signal: Symptoms **began after travel**, a stomach bug, or contaminated water. Chronic loose stool, fatigue, and bloating that never fully resolved after an acute gut illness.

name: H. pylori infection
relevance_signal: **Upper gut symptoms** — nausea, burping, upper abdominal discomfort after eating. Worse with NSAIDs. May have no symptoms at all but contributes to low stomach acid.

name: Fungal overgrowth
relevance_signal: Symptoms began or worsened **after antibiotics**. Bloating, brain fog, fatigue. Cravings for sugar and refined carbohydrates. Thrush or skin fungal infections recurring.

name: Mast cell activation syndrome
relevance_signal: **Multi-system episodes** — gut plus skin flushing plus racing heart plus brain fog simultaneously. Triggers beyond food: heat, stress, smells, pollen. Partial response to antihistamines only.

name: Histamine intolerance
relevance_signal: Histamine intolerance causes wide-ranging symptoms — gut upset, headaches, flushing, hives, runny nose, heart palpitations — that consistently appear after eating aged, fermented, or leftover foods and improve on a low-histamine diet.

name: Nickel sensitivity
relevance_signal: Symptoms worse on a **vegan or wholefood diet** heavy in oats, legumes, nuts, seeds, chocolate. Two-phase reaction: gut symptoms at 1–3h then fatigue and joint aching 24–48h later.

name: IgG food sensitivities
relevance_signal: **Delayed reactions 4–72 hours after eating** — hard to link to a specific food. Symptoms include bloating, fatigue, skin issues, and brain fog with no clear immediate trigger.

name: Non-coeliac gluten sensitivity
relevance_signal: **Symptoms after wheat** without a positive coeliac test. Bloating, gut pain, brain fog, fatigue — improves on gluten-free diet but tTG antibodies are negative.

name: Oxalate accumulation
relevance_signal: Symptoms after **spinach, almonds, dark chocolate, or beets**. Burning sensation. Possible history of kidney stones. Worse on a 'clean eating' diet heavy in superfoods.

name: Salicylate intolerance
relevance_signal: Symptoms after **fresh herbs, berries, tomatoes, and spices** — but not after fermented foods specifically. Overlaps with histamine symptoms but triggered by fresh rather than aged foods.

name: Eosinophilic GI disorders
relevance_signal: **Difficulty swallowing**, food getting stuck, chest pain after eating. Or severe gut pain and vomiting in children. Not responsive to standard IBS treatments.

name: Atopic dermatitis
relevance_signal: **Persistent itchy, dry, inflamed skin** in elbow creases, knees, or face — often dating back to childhood.

name: Allergy
relevance_signal: **Hives, swelling, itching, or breathing trouble** within minutes of contact with a specific food, pollen, or sting.

name: Allergic rhinitis
relevance_signal: **Sneezing, itchy/runny nose, watery eyes** that flare in spring or autumn — or around pets, dust, or mould.

name: Hypermobile EDS / HSD
relevance_signal: **Joints that bend further than normal**, frequent sprains, stretchy skin. Gut symptoms alongside joint pain, fatigue, and POTS or MCAS. Multi-system involvement.

name: POTS / dysautonomia
relevance_signal: **Heart racing on standing**, dizziness, brain fog worse when upright. Gut symptoms worsen 20–30 min after meals regardless of food content. Better lying down.

name: Endometriosis
relevance_signal: **Gut symptoms that worsen around menstruation** — diarrhoea, cramping, bloating, pain on defecation specifically in the days before and during a period.

name: Hormonal / menstrual cycle effects
relevance_signal: Gut symptoms **clearly fluctuate with the menstrual cycle** — worse in the luteal phase (week before period), better mid-cycle. Progesterone slows gut transit.

name: Pelvic floor dysfunction
relevance_signal: **Difficulty completing defecation**, sensation of incomplete emptying, excessive straining. Constipation that does not respond to laxatives or dietary fibre.

name: Gastroparesis
relevance_signal: **Feeling full very quickly**, nausea after meals, upper abdominal bloating that starts during eating rather than hours later. Worse with fatty meals.

name: Surgical adhesions
relevance_signal: **Symptoms began or worsened after abdominal surgery** — appendectomy, C-section, hysterectomy, bowel surgery. Constipation with cramping and a feeling of obstruction.

name: Thoracic outlet syndrome
relevance_signal: **Arm or hand goes numb, tingly, or cold** when raising the arm or carrying weight — often with neck or shoulder pain.

name: Central sensitisation
relevance_signal: **Pain disproportionate to physical findings**. Normal endoscopy but significant gut pain. Symptoms also in other body areas — widespread pain sensitivity, fibromyalgia overlap.

name: Anxiety disorder
relevance_signal: **Gut symptoms clearly worse during stressful periods** — before events, at work, during conflict. Gut and anxiety seem to feed each other. Gut settles when stress resolves.

name: Depression
relevance_signal: **Gut symptoms alongside low mood, low energy, or loss of interest**. Constipation dominant. Symptoms are chronic and pervasive without clear dietary triggers.

name: Trauma / PTSD
relevance_signal: **History of significant trauma** — childhood adversity, abuse, major loss — and IBS that has never responded to dietary or medication approaches. Gut symptoms are treatment-resistant.

name: Fibromyalgia
relevance_signal: **Widespread tender muscles and joints**, unrefreshing sleep, and brain fog alongside the gut symptoms.

name: Functional dyspepsia
relevance_signal: **Pain or fullness in the upper stomach** (not lower), early satiety, or burning — without an ulcer or H. pylori.

name: ADHD
relevance_signal: **Lifelong difficulty focusing, time-blindness, executive-function struggles** — and meals, medications, or sleep are inconsistent.

name: Microscopic colitis
relevance_signal: **Chronic watery diarrhoea** — 5–10 times per day — that looks like IBS-D but does not respond to diet or IBS treatments. Normal colonoscopy appearance but abnormal biopsy.

name: Thyroid dysfunction
relevance_signal: **Constipation with fatigue and weight gain** (hypothyroid) or **diarrhoea with weight loss and anxiety** (hyperthyroid). Gut symptoms are part of a broader systemic picture.

name: Coeliac disease
relevance_signal: **Diarrhoea, bloating, fatigue, and anaemia** — especially if northern European heritage. Symptoms improve on gluten-free diet. May have no gut symptoms at all (silent coeliac). Do not start gluten-free diet before testing.

name: IBD in remission
relevance_signal: **History of confirmed IBD** with ongoing IBS-like symptoms despite being in remission. Or: rectal bleeding, significant weight loss, nocturnal symptoms — these require exclusion of IBD.
